back_translation: Now Ahab told Jezebel everything that Elijah had done, and how he had killed all the prophets with the sword.
So Jezebel sent a messenger to Elijah, saying, “May the gods deal with me, and ever so severely, if by this time tomorrow I have not made your life like the life of one of them!”
And Elijah was afraid, and he got up and ran for his life. When he came to Beersheba in Judah, he left his servant there,

while he himself went a day’s journey into the wilderness. He came and sat down under a broom tree and prayed that he might die, saying, “I have had enough, O LORD. Take my life, for I am no better than my fathers.”
Then he lay down and slept under the broom tree. And suddenly an angel touched him and said, “Get up and eat.”
He looked, and there by his head was a cake of bread baked on hot coals, and a jar of water. So he ate and drank and lay down again.
The angel of the LORD returned a second time, touched him, and said, “Get up and eat, for the journey is too much for you.”
So he got up and ate and drank, and strengthened by that food he traveled forty days and forty nights until he reached Horeb, the mountain of God.
There he entered a cave and spent the night. And behold, the word of the LORD came to him, saying, “What are you doing here, Elijah?”

He replied, “I have been very zealous for the LORD, the God of Hosts, but the Israelites have forsaken Your covenant, torn down Your altars, and killed Your prophets with the sword. I alone am left, and they are seeking my life.”

Then the LORD said, “Go out and stand on the mountain before the LORD, for the LORD is about to pass by.” And a great and mighty wind tore into the mountains and shattered the rocks before the LORD, but the LORD was not in the wind. After the wind there was an earthquake, but the LORD was not in the earthquake.
After the earthquake there was a fire, but the LORD was not in the fire. And after the fire there was a still, small voice in the silence.
When Elijah heard it, he wrapped his face in his cloak, went out, and stood at the mouth of the cave. And behold, a voice came to him and said, “What are you doing here, Elijah?”

He replied, “I have been very zealous for the LORD, the God of Hosts, but the Israelites have forsaken Your covenant, torn down Your altars, and killed Your prophets with the sword. I alone am left, and they are seeking my life.”
The LORD said to him, “Go back the way you came, to the wilderness of Damascus. When you arrive, anoint Hazael as king over Aram.
And anoint Jehu son of Nimshi as king over Israel, and anoint Elisha son of Shaphat from Abel-meholah to be prophet in your place.
Whoever escapes the sword of Hazael, Jehu will kill, and whoever escapes the sword of Jehu, Elisha will kill.
Yet I have reserved seven thousand in Israel—every knee that has not bowed to Baal and every mouth that has not kissed him.”
So Elijah went from there and found Elisha son of Shaphat plowing, with twelve teams of oxen before him, and he was with the twelfth. Elijah passed by him and threw his cloak around him.
So Elisha left the oxen, ran after Elijah, and said, “Please let me kiss my father and mother goodbye, and then I will follow you.” “Go back,” Elijah replied, “for what have I done to you?”

So Elisha turned back, took the pair of oxen and slaughtered them, and using the oxen’s equipment as fuel he boiled the meat and gave it to the people, and they ate. Then he rose, followed Elijah, and served him.
